title: Thinking Machines Lab launches Inkling, an open language model with 975B parameters
summary: The startup founded by Mira Murati publishes its first open-weights model, competing with AI industry leaders.

title: Nvidia and Sega resume their alliance to bring classic games to RTX Spark PCs
summary: The partnership includes the anticipated Virtua Fighter Crossroads and uses Nvidia's RTX Spark technology.

title: 26 former Meta employees sue over AI-assisted mass layoffs
summary: Workers allege that the company used discriminatory AI systems to select whom to lay off, including employees on leave.

title: Apple Intelligence arrives in China with Alibaba's Qwen and is tested in iOS 27 beta
summary: Apple's AI is approved in China through an alliance with Alibaba, while the public beta of iOS 27 integrates the new Siri features.

title: Microsoft fixes a record 622 security vulnerabilities, including two active attacks
summary: The company attributes the increase in patches to the use of artificial intelligence for detecting flaws in its ecosystem.

title: Spotify adds AI chat for Premium subscribers and managed accounts for parents
summary: The streaming platform expands its social features with a conversational assistant and extends parental controls to its free tier.

title: A hack reveals that Suno used YouTube, Deezer, and Genius to train its music AI
summary: Leaked source code shows how the AI music generator extracted decades of songs without explicit permission.

title: SpaceX shares fall below its IPO price of $135
summary: The collapse occurs just before the launch of the Starship spacecraft, creating uncertainty among investors.

title: Stripe and Advent International offer more than $53 billion to buy PayPal
summary: The potential acquisition would be one of the largest in the digital payments sector in recent history.

title: Palit relaunches the GeForce RTX 3060 12 GB graphics card as a solution to the AI crisis
summary: The company presents an updated version of its 2021 GPU to meet the high demand for AI hardware.

title: A modder turns Steam games into physical cartridges using 2.5-inch SSDs
summary: The ingenious system allows storing complete titles on 128GB drives with an auto-start script.

title: Valve stops manufacturing spare parts for the Steam Deck LCD, complicating repairs
summary: The manufacturer's decision makes maintenance of the original model difficult by gradually withdrawing official components.

title: Starlink launches a smaller, lighter V5 residential kit with half the energy consumption
summary: The new antenna offers speeds up to 375 Mbps with a significantly reduced design compared to the previous generation.

title: OpenAI prepares a screenless speaker with ChatGPT for launch in 2027
summary: The company's first own hardware device will be designed to feel alive and operate through voice conversation.

title: Sony plans a digital-only PS6 that would leave 121 countries without store access
summary: Recent patents from the company reveal solutions for PS5 issues while a fully digital ecosystem takes shape.

title: The rise of AI drives up electricity consumption and energy prices globally
summary: The race for artificial intelligence is causing a record increase in electricity consumption and prices, affecting both households and large corporations.

title: A new cell therapy saves four children with terminal brain cancer
summary: The innovative treatment, published in New Scientist, manages to reverse cases considered incurable by conventional medicine.

title: GPT-5.6 Sol solves a 30-year-old unsolved statistical conjecture in 90 minutes
summary: The next-generation AI model achieves what human mathematicians could not in three decades.

title: Intel manufactures the first volume logic chips with ASML's High NA EUV lithography
summary: The US company marks a milestone by qualifying 18A process layers for 0.55 NA scanners, surpassing its competitors.

title: Vint Cerf, co-creator of the internet, works on a plan to release AI agents on the web
summary: The network pioneer seeks a standard that allows autonomous agents to operate safely in the open digital ecosystem.
